license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.v44e8yw
title: Sample preparation for single nuclei sequencing of brain
author: Anna Martinez Casals

steps_raw.instruction: Place a Petri dish on dry ice and transfer the tissue there, cut it using a chilled scalpel. Transfer the tissue into the precooled Dounce homogenizer and keep the homogenizer on ice during the rest of the protocol ( to reduce heat caused by friction). Dounce Tissue Grinder Set with Two Glass Pestles (Wheaton, cat# 357538, https://us.vwr.com/store/product/4832641/dounce-tissue-grinders-wheaton )
Homogenize the tissue with five strokes of the loose pestle, followed by 10–15 strokes of the tight pestle.

Filter the homogenate through a BD Falcon tube with a cell strainer cap. Falcon™ Test Tube with Cell Strainer Snap Cap (Corning, cat# 352235, https://www.fishersci.com/shop/products/falcon-tube-cell-strainer-cap-mesh-size-35um/0877123 )
Transfer the volume to a chilled 1.5 ml eppendorf and centrifuge at 1000 rcf for 00:08:00 at 4 °C .
Remove the supernatant without disrupting the nuclei pellet.
Add 1800 µL nuclei wash and resuspension buffer to the pelleted nuclei and, using a regular-bore pipette tip, gently pipette mix 8-10 times.
Add 200 µL Myelin Removal Beads II to the resuspended nuclei. Mix with a wide-bore pipette tip (no vortex). Incubate for 00:15:00 at 4 °C .

Meanwhile, place LS columns in the magnetic field of a MACS separator each with 3 mL LS Column Calibration Buffer to rinse the columns. QuadroMACS Separator (MACS Separator - magnetic field, Miltenyi biotec, cat# 130-090-976, https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/SE-en/products/macs-cell-separation/separators/quadromacstm-separator-and-starting-kits.html ) MACS MultiStand (Miltenyi Biotec, cat# 130-042-303, https://www.miltenyibiotec.com/SE-en/products/macs-cell-separation/separators/macs-multistand.html )
Using a 10 ml serological pipette, dilute the nuclei suspension with 10 mL Nuclei Wash and Resuspension buffer, gently pipette mix 5 times.
Centrifuge the nuclei at 500 rcf for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Remove the supernatant without disrupting the nuclei pellet and add 2 mL Nuclei Wash and Resuspension buffer.
Apply 1 mL nuclei suspension in each column.
Wash each of the columns with 1 mL Nuclei Wash and Resuspension buffer go to step #13
Collect the effluent into two 5ml Eppendorf tubes.
Centrifuge the two vials with nuclei at 500 rcf for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .
Remove the supernatant from the two vials without disrupting the nuclei pellet and add 500 µL Nuclei Wash and Resuspension buffer.
Pool both resuspended nuclei in a new pre-chilled 1.5 ml Eppendorf.
Centrifuge at 1000 rcf for 00:08:00 at 4 °C .
Aspirate the supernatant (~1,000 μl) and gently resuspend the pellet in 250 μl of homogenization buffer.
Meanwhile, prepare the iodixanol dilution mix (IDM) and iodixanol dilutions to place them on ice.
Strain the mixture through a BD Falcon tube with a cell strainer cap to remove any remaining aggregates, and place it on ice.
Gently mix the nuclei with 250 μl of 50% (vol/vol) iodixanol, and, to a new Eppendorf tube, add 500 μl of 29% iodixanol. Slowly layer 500 μl of the nuclei mixture over the 29% iodixanol.
Centrifuge at 13.500 rcf for 00:20:00 at 4 °C .
Remove and discard the supernatant without disrupting the nuclei pellet.
Resuspend the final pellet in 60 µL Nuclei Wash and Resuspension buffer. The sample is ready for QC-nuclei counting.
